_comment: CloudFront cache TTL configuration for help.nevent.ai. Apply during next deployment. See DEPLOYMENT.md for instructions.
_apply_with: aws s3 sync with --cache-control per file type, or via CloudFront origin response policies

rules.description: HTML pages — short TTL so content updates propagate quickly
Astro hashed assets (JS, CSS) — immutable, served with content hash in filename
Static images — long TTL, rarely change
LLMs index file — short TTL so AI crawlers pick up updates quickly
LLMs full content file — same short TTL as llms.txt
LLMs small content file — same short TTL as llms.txt
LLM content policy — daily refresh sufficient
robots.txt — hourly refresh to pick up crawl rule changes
Sitemap files — hourly refresh so crawlers pick up new pages

implementation_notes: These rules are applied during S3 sync using --cache-control flags per file extension/pattern.
The deployment script (DEPLOYMENT.md, aws-setup.sh) should be updated to pass correct --cache-control per content type.
CloudFront invalidation (aws cloudfront create-invalidation) should be run after each deploy for HTML files.
Hashed assets (_astro/*) do NOT need invalidation — new deploy produces new hashes.
